title: 修复归档覆盖顺序可验证性

sources.reason: Current archive flow moves evidence without recording an explicit monotonic archive order.
PR ownership rank currently falls back to commit timestamp and path order.
Local ownership preview uses the same archive rank for overlapping claims.
Summary validation must accept and validate the archive ordering evidence field.
PR archive owner selection and rank regression tests.
Summary schema compatibility and validation tests.
Defines verifiable archive ownership ordering acceptance.

riskAssessment.reason: Incorrect overlap ordering can assign restricted or generated files to the wrong Work Item; historical archives must remain readable while new archives require explicit ordering evidence.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A newly archived Work Item records one positive archiveSequence in both Contract and Summary.
Overlapping new archive claims are resolved by archiveSequence regardless of same-second commit time or path names.
Missing, duplicate, or invalid new sequence evidence fails closed while legacy archives remain readable.

executionDecision.reason: The existing timestamp/path heuristic is directly visible and can be replaced with sequence-backed ordering plus legacy compatibility.

preReviewWarnings: Verify duplicate, missing, or non-monotonic archive sequence evidence cannot silently decide ownership.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: New archived Work Items receive a positive monotonic archiveSequence recorded in the archived evidence pair.
PR and local ownership ranking use archiveSequence when present and preserve legacy fallback only for historical archives.
Missing, duplicate, or invalid sequence evidence is rejected for new archive overlap decisions.
Same-second archive fixtures prove ownership does not depend on path names or commit timestamps.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Overlapping archived Work Items are currently ordered by second-level commit time followed by archive path, which can assign ownership to the wrong pair when commits share timestamps or paths sort differently from responsibility order.
constraints: Do not rewrite historical archive files solely to add ordering metadata.
New archive ordering must be deterministic and verifiable from repository evidence.
Keep PR and local ownership preview semantics aligned.
intent.rationale: A monotonic sequence assigned at archival time expresses responsibility order directly and removes path names from the authority decision while preserving a bounded compatibility path for legacy records.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly requested the remediation loop to continue until all listed review blockers are completed.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
